meta.name: GAD-2 Generalized Anxiety Disorder Brief
meta.description: The GAD-2 is an ultra-brief 2-item anxiety screening tool derived from the first two items of the GAD-7. It serves as a rapid first-step screening instrument to identify individuals who may benefit from further evaluation with the full GAD-7.
meta.author: Kroenke, Spitzer, Williams & Löwe (2007)
meta.reference: Kroenke K, Spitzer RL, Williams JBW, Monahan PO, Löwe B. Anxiety disorders in primary care: prevalence, impairment, comorbidity, and detection. Ann Intern Med. 2007;146(5):317-325.
meta.instruction: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems?

questions.text: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range. No significant anxiety symptoms detected by this brief screen.
suggestions: Practice relaxation activities for general wellness
Occasional worry is a normal part of life

ranges.label: Possible Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score suggests possible anxiety. This brief screening indicates that further evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Consider completing the full GAD-7 for a more detailed assessment
Consult a healthcare professional for further evaluation
Practice relaxation techniques such as deep breathing or meditation
Monitor your worry patterns and identify triggers
